# Man of the World (MOW)

> Man of the World (MOW) is a Los Angeles creative brand & marketing agency. We make bold unforgettable — unearthing a company's core truth and expressing it with clarity, conviction, and cultural authority across AI, healthcare, climate tech, infrastructure, and culture.

## About
- Name: Man of the World (also "MOW", "MOW Media")
- Founder & CEO: Peter Weltman
- Location: Los Angeles, California, USA (working across the U.S., Latin America, and Canada)
- Contact: peter@mow.media · (424) 460-0537
- Tagline: Make bold unforgettable
- Positioning: A journalistic, luxury-craft, AI-forward branding practice for high-stakes moments — rebrands, M&A, spinoffs, post-bankruptcy relaunches, and category-defining launches.

## What MOW does
- Brand strategy, naming, messaging, and identity systems
- Custom websites and UX
- SEO, Answer Engine Optimization (AEO), and Generative Engine Optimization (GEO)
- Generative-AI marketing and launch films
- Executive & thought-leadership branding

## Industries
AI & emerging tech; healthcare & precision medicine; infrastructure, energy & climate; consumer platforms & urban tech; media, publishing & culture.

## Impossible to Ignore (i2i)
MOW's visual philosophy and global brand-distinctiveness standard. Seven observable tenets: Luxury in the Details; Distinctiveness Over Conventional Beauty; Intentionally Incomplete; Sprezzatura; Absolute Conviction; Authenticity of Experience; and Koochooloo (کوچولو). The thesis: the world does not need more content — it needs better-trained attention. Keep the standard identical; let the proof stay local.

## Selected work
- Caspian Coast Coffee — a Persian café in Venice, CA (Venice style, Persian soul).
- All Model Repair — Glendale's most trusted neighborhood auto shop, in English & Farsi, with an SEO/AEO/GEO strategy.
- Bananos Inteligentes — the boldest rebrand in the banana's history; a unified data-intelligence platform (Mérida, México).
- Petra — autonomous tunneling; brand behind a $33M raise with CNBC, Forbes, WIRED & TechCrunch coverage.
- Avitia — AI cancer-testing brand relaunched out of bankruptcy; expanded to Canada, Thailand & the Middle East.
- AutoAlign · Sidecar — generative-AI security spinoff using NVIDIA NeMo Guardrails; GTM with KPMG.
- Hey Houston — a hip, tech-forward community events platform.
- Pedrera — the identity of a $1B+ mixed-use urban landmark in Guatemala City.
- Neara — U.S. launch of an AI climate-tech platform; $24M raise, Southern California Edison partnership, >$1B valuation.
